Compare Python vs MATLAB Fusion Results¶

Run this after:

  1. Python: run_fusion_isoview.ipynb (output in E:\isoview\multifused)
  2. MATLAB: matlab\run_fusion.m (output in E:\isoview\matlab)
In [34]:
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pyklb

plt.rcParams['figure.dpi'] = 100

# paths
PYTHON_DIR = Path(r"E:\isoview\multifused")
MATLAB_BASE = Path(r"E:\isoview")  # matlab outputs to subdirs here
TP = 0

print("Python dir:", PYTHON_DIR)
print("MATLAB base:", MATLAB_BASE)

Python dir: E:\isoview\multifused
MATLAB base: E:\isoview
In [35]:
def find_matlab_result(cameras, channel):
    """Find MATLAB fusion result - searches common output patterns."""
    # matlab output naming: {base}.TM000000_multiFused{id}/SPM00_TM...fusedStack.klb
    patterns = [
        # current output structure
        f"*multiFused*cam{cameras[0]}{cameras[1]}*/SPM00_TM{TP:06d}_CM{cameras[0]:02d}_CM{cameras[1]:02d}_CHN{channel:02d}.fusedStack.klb",
        f"*multiFused*/SPM00_TM{TP:06d}_CM{cameras[0]:02d}_CM{cameras[1]:02d}_CHN{channel:02d}.fusedStack.klb",
        f"matlab*/SPM00_TM{TP:06d}_CM{cameras[0]:02d}_CM{cameras[1]:02d}_CHN{channel:02d}.fusedStack.klb",
    ]
    for pattern in patterns:
        matches = list(MATLAB_BASE.glob(pattern))
        if matches:
            return matches[0]
    return None

def load_results(cameras, channel):
    """Load Python and MATLAB results for a camera pair."""
    # python
    py_path = PYTHON_DIR / f"SPM00_TM{TP:06d}_CM{cameras[0]:02d}_CM{cameras[1]:02d}_CHN{channel:02d}.fusedStack.klb"
    py_vol = pyklb.readfull(str(py_path)) if py_path.exists() else None

    # matlab
    mat_path = find_matlab_result(cameras, channel)
    mat_vol = pyklb.readfull(str(mat_path)) if mat_path else None

    return py_vol, mat_vol, py_path, mat_path

# list what we have
print("Python results:")
for f in sorted(PYTHON_DIR.glob("*.fusedStack.klb")):
    print(f"  {f.name}")

print("\nMATLAB results:")
for f in sorted(MATLAB_BASE.glob("*multiFused*/*.fusedStack.klb")):
    print(f"  {f.parent.name}/{f.name}")

Python results:
  SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.fusedStack.klb
  SPM00_TM000000_CM02_CM03_CHN00.fusedStack.klb

MATLAB results:
  matlab.TM000000_multiFused_cam01/SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.fusedStack.klb
  matlab.TM000000_multiFused_cam23/SPM00_TM000000_CM02_CM03_CHN00.fusedStack.klb
  multifused/SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.fusedStack.klb
  multifused/SPM00_TM000000_CM02_CM03_CHN00.fusedStack.klb

Compare Camera 0+1 (CHN01)¶

In [36]:
py_01, mat_01, py_path_01, mat_path_01 = load_results([0, 1], 1)

print("Camera 0+1 (CHN01):")
print(f"  Python: {py_path_01}")
print(f"  MATLAB: {mat_path_01}")

if py_01 is not None:
    print(f"  Python shape: {py_01.shape}, range: [{py_01.min()}, {py_01.max()}]")
if mat_01 is not None:
    print(f"  MATLAB shape: {mat_01.shape}, range: [{mat_01.min()}, {mat_01.max()}]")

Camera 0+1 (CHN01):
  Python: E:\isoview\multifused\SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.fusedStack.klb
  MATLAB: E:\isoview\matlab.TM000000_multiFused_cam01\SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.fusedStack.klb
  Python shape: (79, 2048, 752), range: [0, 1105]
  MATLAB shape: (79, 2048, 752), range: [0, 1047]
In [37]:
if py_01 is not None and mat_01 is not None:
    y_mid = py_01.shape[1] // 2

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(15, 10))

    # top row: XZ slices
    axes[0,0].imshow(py_01[:, y_mid, :], cmap='gray', aspect='auto')
    axes[0,0].set_title(f'Python (range: {py_01.min()}-{py_01.max()})')

    axes[0,1].imshow(mat_01[:, y_mid, :], cmap='gray', aspect='auto')
    axes[0,1].set_title(f'MATLAB (range: {mat_01.min()}-{mat_01.max()})')

    # difference
    if py_01.shape == mat_01.shape:
        diff = py_01.astype(float) - mat_01.astype(float)
        im = axes[0,2].imshow(diff[:, y_mid, :], cmap='RdBu', aspect='auto',
                              vmin=-diff.max(), vmax=diff.max())
        plt.colorbar(im, ax=axes[0,2])
        axes[0,2].set_title(f'Difference (max={np.abs(diff).max():.1f})')

    # bottom row: line profiles
    z_mid = py_01.shape[0] // 2
    axes[1,0].plot(py_01[z_mid, y_mid, :], 'b-', label='Python', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,0].plot(mat_01[z_mid, y_mid, :], 'r--', label='MATLAB', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,0].legend()
    axes[1,0].set_title('X profile')
    axes[1,0].set_xlabel('X')

    axes[1,1].plot(py_01[:, y_mid, py_01.shape[2]//2], 'b-', label='Python', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,1].plot(mat_01[:, y_mid, mat_01.shape[2]//2], 'r--', label='MATLAB', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,1].legend()
    axes[1,1].set_title('Z profile')
    axes[1,1].set_xlabel('Z')

    # stats
    if py_01.shape == mat_01.shape:
        corr = np.corrcoef(py_01.ravel(), mat_01.ravel())[0,1]
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.8, f'Correlation: {corr:.6f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.6, f'Max diff: {np.abs(diff).max():.2f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.4, f'Mean diff: {np.abs(diff).mean():.2f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.2, f'Std diff: {diff.std():.2f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].axis('off')

    plt.suptitle('Camera 0+1 (CHN01) Comparison', fontsize=14)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("Missing data for comparison")

    axes[0,1].imshow(mat_23[:, y_mid, :], cmap='gray', aspect='auto')

                              vmin=-diff.max(), vmax=diff.max())

        axes[0,2].set_title(f'Difference (max={np.abs(diff).max():.1f})')

    # bottom row: line profiles

    axes[1,0].plot(mat_23[z_mid, y_mid, :], 'r--', label='MATLAB', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,0].legend()
    axes[1,0].set_title('X profile')
    axes[1,0].set_xlabel('X')

    axes[1,1].plot(py_23[:, y_mid, py_23.shape[2]//2], 'b-', label='Python', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,1].plot(mat_23[:, y_mid, mat_23.shape[2]//2], 'r--', label='MATLAB', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,1].legend()
    axes[1,1].set_title('Z profile')
    axes[1,1].set_xlabel('Z')

    # stats
    if py_23.shape == mat_23.shape:
        corr = np.corrcoef(py_23.ravel(), mat_23.ravel())[0,1]
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.8, f'Correlation: {corr:.6f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.6, f'Max diff: {np.abs(diff).max():.2f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.4, f'Mean diff: {np.abs(diff).mean():.2f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.2, f'Std diff: {diff.std():.2f}', transform=axes[1,2].transAxes, fontsize=12)
        axes[1,2].axis('off')

    plt.suptitle('Camera 2+3 (CHN00) Comparison', fontsize=14)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("Missing data for comparison")

Detailed Difference Analysis¶

In [40]:
def analyze_diff(py_vol, mat_vol, name):
    """Detailed difference analysis."""
    if py_vol is None or mat_vol is None:
        print(f"{name}: Missing data")
        return

    if py_vol.shape != mat_vol.shape:
        print(f"{name}: Shape mismatch - Python {py_vol.shape} vs MATLAB {mat_vol.shape}")
        return

    diff = py_vol.astype(float) - mat_vol.astype(float)

    print(f"\n{name}:")
    print(f"  Shape: {py_vol.shape}")
    print(f"  Python - min: {py_vol.min()}, max: {py_vol.max()}, mean: {py_vol.mean():.2f}")
    print(f"  MATLAB - min: {mat_vol.min()}, max: {mat_vol.max()}, mean: {mat_vol.mean():.2f}")
    print(f"  Difference - min: {diff.min():.2f}, max: {diff.max():.2f}, mean: {diff.mean():.2f}, std: {diff.std():.2f}")
    print(f"  Correlation: {np.corrcoef(py_vol.ravel(), mat_vol.ravel())[0,1]:.6f}")

    # where are the biggest differences?
    abs_diff = np.abs(diff)
    max_idx = np.unravel_index(abs_diff.argmax(), abs_diff.shape)
    print(f"  Max diff location: z={max_idx[0]}, y={max_idx[1]}, x={max_idx[2]}")
    print(f"  At max diff - Python: {py_vol[max_idx]}, MATLAB: {mat_vol[max_idx]}")

analyze_diff(py_01, mat_01, "Camera 0+1")
analyze_diff(py_23, mat_23, "Camera 2+3")

In [41]:
# analyze zero patterns - MATLAB may zero out regions outside fusion mask
print("Zero pattern analysis:")
print(f"\nCamera 0+1:")
print(f"  Python zeros: {(py_01 == 0).sum()} ({100*(py_01 == 0).mean():.1f}%)")
print(f"  MATLAB zeros: {(mat_01 == 0).sum()} ({100*(mat_01 == 0).mean():.1f}%)")
print(f"  Python>0 but MATLAB=0: {((py_01 > 0) & (mat_01 == 0)).sum()}")
print(f"  Python=0 but MATLAB>0: {((py_01 == 0) & (mat_01 > 0)).sum()}")

print(f"\nCamera 2+3:")
print(f"  Python zeros: {(py_23 == 0).sum()} ({100*(py_23 == 0).mean():.1f}%)")
print(f"  MATLAB zeros: {(mat_23 == 0).sum()} ({100*(mat_23 == 0).mean():.1f}%)")
print(f"  Python>0 but MATLAB=0: {((py_23 > 0) & (mat_23 == 0)).sum()}")
print(f"  Python=0 but MATLAB>0: {((py_23 == 0) & (mat_23 > 0)).sum()}")

# look at max diff locations
print("\n--- Max diff location analysis ---")
z, y, x = 76, 1978, 304
print(f"\nCam0+1 max diff at z={z}, y={y}, x={x}:")
print(f"  Python: {py_01[z, y, x]}, MATLAB: {mat_01[z, y, x]}")
print(f"  Neighborhood (z, y-5:y+5, x):")
print(f"    Python: {py_01[z, max(0,y-5):y+5, x]}")
print(f"    MATLAB: {mat_01[z, max(0,y-5):y+5, x]}")

z, y, x = 70, 1052, 192
print(f"\nCam2+3 max diff at z={z}, y={y}, x={x}:")
print(f"  Python: {py_23[z, y, x]}, MATLAB: {mat_23[z, y, x]}")
print(f"  Neighborhood (z-2:z+3, y, x):")
print(f"    Python: {py_23[max(0,z-2):z+3, y, x]}")
print(f"    MATLAB: {mat_23[max(0,z-2):z+3, y, x]}")

In [42]:
# investigate why MATLAB zeros out x>570 region
# look at the 2D (X, Z) zero pattern for MATLAB

z_mid = mat_01.shape[0] // 2
y_mid = mat_01.shape[1] // 2

# check if MATLAB is zeroing entire X columns or specific (x, z) positions
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(15, 10))

# show MATLAB zeros in 2D (X, Z) at y_mid
mat_xz = mat_01[:, y_mid, :]
py_xz = py_01[:, y_mid, :]
axes[0,0].imshow((mat_xz == 0).T, cmap='gray', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[0,0].set_title('MATLAB zeros at y=1024 (white=zero)')
axes[0,0].set_xlabel('Z'); axes[0,0].set_ylabel('X')

axes[0,1].imshow((py_xz == 0).T, cmap='gray', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[0,1].set_title('Python zeros at y=1024 (white=zero)')
axes[0,1].set_xlabel('Z'); axes[0,1].set_ylabel('X')

# show where they differ
diff_zeros = (mat_xz == 0) != (py_xz == 0)
axes[0,2].imshow(diff_zeros.T, cmap='Reds', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[0,2].set_title('Zero pattern difference')
axes[0,2].set_xlabel('Z'); axes[0,2].set_ylabel('X')

# check X profile at different Z values
for i, z in enumerate([30, 50, 70]):
    axes[1,i].plot(py_01[z, y_mid, :], 'b-', label='Python', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,i].plot(mat_01[z, y_mid, :], 'r--', label='MATLAB', alpha=0.7)
    axes[1,i].legend()
    axes[1,i].set_title(f'X profile at z={z}')
    axes[1,i].set_xlabel('X')
    # mark where MATLAB goes to zero but Python doesn't
    x_diff = np.where((py_01[z, y_mid, :] > 0) & (mat_01[z, y_mid, :] == 0))[0]
    if len(x_diff) > 0:
        axes[1,i].axvline(x_diff[0], color='g', linestyle=':', label=f'MATLAB zeros at x={x_diff[0]}')

plt.suptitle('Cam0+1: Investigating MATLAB zero pattern', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# find the X boundary where MATLAB starts zeroing
print("\nWhere does MATLAB start zeroing for different Z slices?")
for z in [30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 75]:
    mat_line = mat_01[z, y_mid, :]
    py_line = py_01[z, y_mid, :]
    # find first x where python > 0 but matlab = 0
    diff_x = np.where((py_line > 0) & (mat_line == 0))[0]
    if len(diff_x) > 0:
        print(f"  z={z}: MATLAB zeros start at x={diff_x[0]}, Python value there: {py_line[diff_x[0]]}")
    else:
        print(f"  z={z}: No zero difference found")

Where does MATLAB start zeroing for different Z slices?
  z=30: MATLAB zeros start at x=36, Python value there: 10
  z=40: MATLAB zeros start at x=36, Python value there: 26
  z=50: MATLAB zeros start at x=36, Python value there: 1
  z=60: MATLAB zeros start at x=36, Python value there: 9
  z=70: MATLAB zeros start at x=36, Python value there: 10
  z=75: MATLAB zeros start at x=36, Python value there: 7
In [43]:
# load MATLAB's saved averageMask to compare with Python
matlab_mask_path = Path(r"E:\isoview\matlab.TM000000_multiFused_cam01\SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.mask.klb")
matlab_avg_mask = pyklb.readfull(str(matlab_mask_path))

print(f"MATLAB averageMask shape: {matlab_avg_mask.shape}")
print(f"MATLAB averageMask range: [{matlab_avg_mask.min()}, {matlab_avg_mask.max()}]")
print(f"MATLAB averageMask non-zero: {(matlab_avg_mask > 0).sum()} ({100*(matlab_avg_mask > 0).mean():.1f}%)")

# visualize the mask
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 5))

# matlab mask is (X, Z)
axes[0].imshow(matlab_avg_mask.T, cmap='viridis', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[0].set_title(f'MATLAB averageMask (X, Z)')
axes[0].set_xlabel('X'); axes[0].set_ylabel('Z')
axes[0].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--', label='x=570')

# show where mask is zero
axes[1].imshow((matlab_avg_mask == 0).T, cmap='gray', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[1].set_title('MATLAB mask zeros (white=zero)')
axes[1].set_xlabel('X'); axes[1].set_ylabel('Z')
axes[1].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')

# X profile of where mask is zero
z_profile = (matlab_avg_mask > 0).sum(axis=1)  # sum over Z for each X
axes[2].plot(z_profile)
axes[2].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--', label='x=570')
axes[2].set_title('# of Z slices with mask > 0 per X')
axes[2].set_xlabel('X')
axes[2].legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# find where mask becomes zero
print("\nMATLAB mask coverage along X:")
for x in [400, 500, 550, 570, 600, 650, 700]:
    z_nonzero = (matlab_avg_mask[x, :] > 0).sum()
    print(f"  x={x}: {z_nonzero}/79 Z slices have mask > 0")

MATLAB averageMask range: [0, 70]
MATLAB averageMask non-zero: 1201528 (78.0%)

  x=500: 622/79 Z slices have mask > 0

  x=570: 662/79 Z slices have mask > 0
  x=600: 670/79 Z slices have mask > 0
  x=650: 659/79 Z slices have mask > 0
  x=700: 678/79 Z slices have mask > 0
In [44]:
# load MATLAB's saved masks to understand the zero pattern
matlab_mask_dir = Path(r"E:\isoview\matlab.TM000000_multiFused_cam01")

# load individual camera masks and the combined mask
cam0_mask2d = pyklb.readfull(str(matlab_mask_dir / "SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CHN01.mask2D.klb"))
cam1_mask2d = pyklb.readfull(str(matlab_mask_dir / "SPM00_TM000000_CM01_CHN01.mask2D.klb"))
cam1_transformed = pyklb.readfull(str(matlab_mask_dir / "SPM00_TM000000_CM01_CHN01.transformedMask2D.klb"))
combined_mask = pyklb.readfull(str(matlab_mask_dir / "SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.mask.klb"))
fusion_mask = pyklb.readfull(str(matlab_mask_dir / "SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CM01_CHN01.fusionMask.klb"))

print("MATLAB mask shapes:")
print(f"  cam0 mask2D: {cam0_mask2d.shape}")
print(f"  cam1 mask2D: {cam1_mask2d.shape}")
print(f"  cam1 transformed: {cam1_transformed.shape}")
print(f"  combined mask: {combined_mask.shape}")
print(f"  fusion mask: {fusion_mask.shape}")

# visualize
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(15, 10))

axes[0,0].imshow(cam0_mask2d.T, cmap='viridis', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[0,0].set_title('cam0 sliceMask (before transform)')
axes[0,0].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')

axes[0,1].imshow(cam1_mask2d.T, cmap='viridis', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[0,1].set_title('cam1 sliceMask (before transform)')
axes[0,1].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')

axes[0,2].imshow(cam1_transformed.T, cmap='viridis', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[0,2].set_title('cam1 sliceMask (after transform)')
axes[0,2].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')

axes[1,0].imshow(combined_mask.T, cmap='viridis', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[1,0].set_title('combined averageMask')
axes[1,0].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')

axes[1,1].imshow((combined_mask == 0).T, cmap='gray', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[1,1].set_title('averageMask zeros (white=zero)')
axes[1,1].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')

# show where cam0 has signal but combined is zero
cam0_has = cam0_mask2d > 0
combined_zero = combined_mask == 0
lost_signal = cam0_has & combined_zero
axes[1,2].imshow(lost_signal.T, cmap='Reds', aspect='auto', origin='lower')
axes[1,2].set_title('cam0 has signal but combined=0 (red)')
axes[1,2].axvline(570, color='b', linestyle='--')

plt.suptitle('MATLAB masks for cam0+1', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# check coverage
print(f"\nMask coverage:")
print(f"  cam0 mask2D non-zero: {(cam0_mask2d > 0).mean()*100:.1f}%")
print(f"  cam1 mask2D non-zero: {(cam1_mask2d > 0).mean()*100:.1f}%")
print(f"  cam1 transformed non-zero: {(cam1_transformed > 0).mean()*100:.1f}%")
print(f"  combined mask non-zero: {(combined_mask > 0).mean()*100:.1f}%")
print(f"  fusion mask non-zero: {(fusion_mask > 0).mean()*100:.1f}%")

print(f"\nAt x=600:")
print(f"  cam0 mask2D: {(cam0_mask2d[600, :] > 0).sum()}/79 Z slices")
print(f"  cam1 transformed: {(cam1_transformed[600, :] > 0).sum()}/79 Z slices")
print(f"  combined: {(combined_mask[600, :] > 0).sum()}/79 Z slices")

MATLAB mask shapes:
  cam0 mask2D: (2048, 752)
  cam1 mask2D: (2048, 752)
  cam1 transformed: (2048, 752)
  combined mask: (2048, 752)
  fusion mask: (2048, 752)

Mask coverage:
  cam0 mask2D non-zero: 68.7%
  cam1 mask2D non-zero: 77.4%
  cam1 transformed non-zero: 73.2%
  combined mask non-zero: 78.0%
  fusion mask non-zero: 74.4%

At x=600:
  cam0 mask2D: 542/79 Z slices
  cam1 transformed: 642/79 Z slices
  combined: 670/79 Z slices
In [45]:
# the masks are (Y, X) not (X, Z)!
# MATLAB stores Z-coordinate of transition for each (y, x) position
# let me re-interpret

print("Re-interpreting mask dimensions:")
print(f"  Volume shape: (Z=79, Y=2048, X=752)")
print(f"  Mask shape: {combined_mask.shape} = (Y=2048, X=752)")
print(f"  So mask stores Z-transition value for each (y, x) pixel")

# the "lost signal" corner is at high Y and high X
# let's check the values there
print(f"\nMask values at corner (y=1900-2000, x=700-750):")
print(f"  cam0 mask range: {cam0_mask2d[1900:2000, 700:750].min()} - {cam0_mask2d[1900:2000, 700:750].max()}")
print(f"  cam1 transformed range: {cam1_transformed[1900:2000, 700:750].min()} - {cam1_transformed[1900:2000, 700:750].max()}")
print(f"  combined range: {combined_mask[1900:2000, 700:750].min()} - {combined_mask[1900:2000, 700:750].max()}")

# check if cam0 has signal there
print(f"\n  cam0 has signal at (y=1950, x=720): {cam0_mask2d[1950, 720]}")
print(f"  cam1 transformed at (y=1950, x=720): {cam1_transformed[1950, 720]}")
print(f"  combined at (y=1950, x=720): {combined_mask[1950, 720]}")

# the issue: when fusionType=1 and transitions=70, MATLAB sets averageMask(averageMask > 0) = 70
# but if combined_mask is 0 at that location, it stays 0 and that pixel is zeroed in output

# check what fusion_mask looks like - this might be the actual mask used
print(f"\nFusion mask at corner:")
print(f"  fusion_mask at (y=1950, x=720): {fusion_mask[1950, 720]}")
print(f"  fusion_mask range: {fusion_mask.min()} - {fusion_mask.max()}")

# compare fusion_mask coverage vs combined_mask
print(f"\nMask coverage comparison:")
print(f"  combined_mask > 0: {(combined_mask > 0).sum()} pixels ({(combined_mask > 0).mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"  fusion_mask > 0: {(fusion_mask > 0).sum()} pixels ({(fusion_mask > 0).mean()*100:.1f}%)")

Re-interpreting mask dimensions:
  Volume shape: (Z=79, Y=2048, X=752)
  Mask shape: (2048, 752) = (Y=2048, X=752)
  So mask stores Z-transition value for each (y, x) pixel

Mask values at corner (y=1900-2000, x=700-750):
  cam0 mask range: 0 - 0
  cam1 transformed range: 0 - 0
  combined range: 0 - 0

  cam0 has signal at (y=1950, x=720): 0
  cam1 transformed at (y=1950, x=720): 0
  combined at (y=1950, x=720): 0

Fusion mask at corner:
  fusion_mask at (y=1950, x=720): 0
  fusion_mask range: 0 - 70

Mask coverage comparison:
  combined_mask > 0: 1201528 pixels (78.0%)
  fusion_mask > 0: 1145798 pixels (74.4%)
In [46]:
# Focus on the X profile difference pattern
# At z=39 (z_mid), y=1024 (y_mid), X profile shows MATLAB drops but mask is valid

z_mid = 39
y_mid = 1024

# find exact X where MATLAB drops to zero but Python doesn't
py_line = py_01[z_mid, y_mid, :]
mat_line = mat_01[z_mid, y_mid, :]

# find where they diverge
diverge_x = np.where((py_line > 10) & (mat_line == 0))[0]
print(f"X positions where Python>10 but MATLAB=0 at z={z_mid}, y={y_mid}:")
if len(diverge_x) > 0:
    print(f"  First: x={diverge_x[0]}, Last: x={diverge_x[-1]}, Count: {len(diverge_x)}")
else:
    print("  None found!")
    # maybe MATLAB isn't zero, just different - check where diff is large
    diff_line = np.abs(py_line.astype(float) - mat_line.astype(float))
    large_diff = np.where(diff_line > 10)[0]
    print(f"  Large diff (>10) at: {large_diff[:20]}...")

# plot detailed comparison
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))

# full X profile
axes[0,0].plot(py_line, 'b-', label='Python', linewidth=1)
axes[0,0].plot(mat_line, 'r--', label='MATLAB', linewidth=1)
axes[0,0].legend()
axes[0,0].set_title(f'Full X profile at z={z_mid}, y={y_mid}')
axes[0,0].set_xlabel('X')

# zoomed to x=500-752
axes[0,1].plot(range(500, 752), py_line[500:752], 'b-', label='Python', linewidth=1)
axes[0,1].plot(range(500, 752), mat_line[500:752], 'r--', label='MATLAB', linewidth=1)
axes[0,1].legend()
axes[0,1].set_title(f'X profile x=500-752 at z={z_mid}, y={y_mid}')
axes[0,1].set_xlabel('X')

# check at different Z values
for i, z in enumerate([30, 75]):
    py_z = py_01[z, y_mid, :]
    mat_z = mat_01[z, y_mid, :]
    axes[1,i].plot(py_z, 'b-', label='Python', linewidth=1)
    axes[1,i].plot(mat_z, 'r--', label='MATLAB', linewidth=1)
    axes[1,i].legend()
    axes[1,i].set_title(f'X profile at z={z}, y={y_mid}')
    axes[1,i].set_xlabel('X')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# print actual values at key X positions
print(f"\nDetailed values at z={z_mid}, y={y_mid}:")
for x in range(560, 580):
    print(f"  x={x}: Python={py_line[x]}, MATLAB={mat_line[x]}, diff={int(py_line[x])-int(mat_line[x])}")

In [47]:
# The difference is at z > 70 (above transition, using cam1)
# At z < 70, Python and MATLAB match perfectly (diff = -1)
# At z > 70, they diverge significantly

# Check MATLAB's intensity correction - it saves this to a file
import scipy.io as sio

# look for intensity correction file
correction_files = list(Path(r"E:\isoview\matlab.TM000000_multiFused_cam01").glob("*intensity*"))
print("Intensity correction files:")
for f in correction_files:
    print(f"  {f.name}")

# also check transformation files
transform_files = list(Path(r"E:\isoview\matlab.TM000000_multiFused_cam01").glob("*transform*"))
print("\nTransformation files:")
for f in transform_files:
    print(f"  {f.name}")

# load and check
for f in correction_files:
    if f.suffix == '.mat':
        data = sio.loadmat(str(f))
        print(f"\n{f.name}:")
        for key in data:
            if not key.startswith('_'):
                print(f"  {key}: {data[key]}")

# Compare with Python's correction factor
print(f"\nPython intensity correction factor: 1.662 (from notebook output)")
print("Check if MATLAB's factor matches...")

# check at the MAX DIFF location: z=76, y=1978, x=304
# where Python=1052, MATLAB=0

z_check, y_check, x_check = 76, 1978, 304

print(f"Investigating max diff location: z={z_check}, y={y_check}, x={x_check}")
print(f"  Python output: {py_01[z_check, y_check, x_check]}")
print(f"  MATLAB output: {mat_01[z_check, y_check, x_check]}")

print(f"\nMATLAB mask at (y={y_check}, x={x_check}):")
print(f"  cam0_mask2d: {cam0_mask2d[y_check, x_check]}")
print(f"  cam1_transformed: {cam1_transformed[y_check, x_check]}")
print(f"  combined_mask: {combined_mask[y_check, x_check]}")
print(f"  fusion_mask: {fusion_mask[y_check, x_check]}")

# z=76 is ABOVE the transition z=70
# the mask stores the transition Z, but the output at z=76 depends on whether z=76 > mask value
print(f"\nTransition logic:")
print(f"  z={z_check} is {'above' if z_check > 70 else 'below'} transition z=70")
print(f"  mask value at this (y,x) = {fusion_mask[y_check, x_check]}")
print(f"  Since z > transition, this pixel should come from cam1")

# check raw values
print(f"\nRaw input values at z={z_check}, y={y_check}, x={x_check}:")
print(f"  cam0 raw: {cam0_raw[z_check, y_check, x_check]}")
print(f"  cam1 flipped: {cam1_flipped[z_check, y_check, x_check]}")

# check nearby pixels in the mask
print(f"\nMask values in neighborhood (y={y_check-5}:{y_check+5}, x={x_check}):")
for y in range(y_check-5, y_check+6):
    print(f"  y={y}: fusion_mask={fusion_mask[y, x_check]}, combined={combined_mask[y, x_check]}")

In [ ]:
# load raw input data to check if there's signal at x>570
import pyklb

input_dir = Path(r"E:\isoview\SPM00\TM000000")
cam0_raw = pyklb.readfull(str(input_dir / "SPM00_TM000000_CM00_CHN01.klb"))
cam1_raw = pyklb.readfull(str(input_dir / "SPM00_TM000000_CM01_CHN01.klb"))

y_mid = cam0_raw.shape[1] // 2
z_mid = cam0_raw.shape[0] // 2

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))

# raw data X profiles
axes[0,0].plot(cam0_raw[z_mid, y_mid, :], 'b-', label='cam0 raw')
axes[0,0].plot(cam1_raw[z_mid, y_mid, :], 'r-', label='cam1 raw', alpha=0.7)
axes[0,0].axvline(570, color='g', linestyle='--', label='x=570')
axes[0,0].legend()
axes[0,0].set_title('Raw data X profile at z=39')
axes[0,0].set_xlabel('X')

# cam1 needs to be flipped
cam1_flipped = cam1_raw[:, :, ::-1]
axes[0,1].plot(cam0_raw[z_mid, y_mid, :], 'b-', label='cam0 raw')
axes[0,1].plot(cam1_flipped[z_mid, y_mid, :], 'r-', label='cam1 flipped', alpha=0.7)
axes[0,1].axvline(570, color='g', linestyle='--', label='x=570')
axes[0,1].legend()
axes[0,1].set_title('Raw data X profile (cam1 flipped)')
axes[0,1].set_xlabel('X')

# check what happens at x=600
axes[1,0].imshow(cam0_raw[:, y_mid, :], cmap='gray', aspect='auto')
axes[1,0].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')
axes[1,0].set_title('cam0 raw XZ slice')

axes[1,1].imshow(cam1_flipped[:, y_mid, :], cmap='gray', aspect='auto')
axes[1,1].axvline(570, color='r', linestyle='--')
axes[1,1].set_title('cam1 flipped XZ slice')

plt.suptitle('Raw input data - checking signal at x>570', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# check intensity values at x=600
print(f"\nRaw intensities at x=600, z=39, y={y_mid}:")
print(f"  cam0: {cam0_raw[z_mid, y_mid, 600]}")
print(f"  cam1 flipped: {cam1_flipped[z_mid, y_mid, 600]}")
print(f"\nRaw intensities at x=700, z=39, y={y_mid}:")
print(f"  cam0: {cam0_raw[z_mid, y_mid, 700]}")
print(f"  cam1 flipped: {cam1_flipped[z_mid, y_mid, 700]}")

Raw intensities at x=600, z=39, y=1024:
  cam0: 182
  cam1 flipped: 158

Raw intensities at x=700, z=39, y=1024:
  cam0: 252
  cam1 flipped: 163
